---
title: "02 — Clean & filter articles"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
PROJECT_ROOT <- Sys.getenv("CAPLAN_ROOT")
if (PROJECT_ROOT == "") PROJECT_ROOT <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("_config.R"))
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = PROJECT_ROOT)
source(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "_config.R"))
source(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "R", "helpers.R"))
library(arrow); library(data.table)
```

Cleans the raw per-year Parquet into a partitioned dataset `data_parquet/articles_cleaned/`
(one part per year). Steps: ensure a stable `article_id`, normalize whitespace, compute an
OCR-quality proxy, drop unusably short/noisy articles, and stamp `year`.

## Inspect the raw schema first

```{r schema}
raw_files <- list.files(DATA_PARQUET, pattern = "^articles_\\d{4}\\.parquet$",
                        full.names = TRUE)
stopifnot(length(raw_files) > 0)
print(names(read_parquet(raw_files[1], as_data_frame = FALSE)$schema))
# American Stories typically exposes: article_id, newspaper_name, edition, date,
# page, headline, byline, article (text), and an lccn-bearing id. Adjust the
# column names below if your snapshot differs.
TEXT_COL <- "article"      # main body text
ID_COL   <- "article_id"   # stable id (created from a hash if absent)
```

## Clean, filter, partition

```{r clean}
cleaned_dir <- file.path(DATA_PARQUET, "articles_cleaned")
dir.create(cleaned_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Tunable filters (record chosen values in docs/PROJECT_PLAN.md if changed)
MIN_WORDS   <- 25      # drop fragments
MAX_NONALPHA <- 0.40   # drop badly OCR'd scans

# Optional year scope (CAPLAN_CLEAN_YEARS, e.g. "1895-1945"); default = all ingested years.
.sel <- Sys.getenv("CAPLAN_CLEAN_YEARS")
if (nzchar(.sel)) {
  .keep <- unlist(lapply(strsplit(.sel, ",")[[1]], function(p)
    if (grepl("-", p)) do.call(seq, as.list(as.integer(strsplit(p, "-")[[1]]))) else as.integer(p)))
  .yrs <- as.integer(gsub(".*articles_(\\d{4})\\.parquet", "\\1", raw_files))
  .pick <- .yrs %in% .keep
  raw_files <- raw_files[.pick]; .yrs <- .yrs[.pick]
  raw_files <- raw_files[order(match(.yrs, .keep))]   # honor CAPLAN_CLEAN_YEARS order (e.g. window-first)
}
message("cleaning ", length(raw_files), " year files (parallel)")

# Clean one year: same logic as the validated pipeline (normalize, quality-filter,
# exact-dup drop), with an atomic write so a killed worker can't leave a partial file.
clean_one <- function(rf) {
  yr <- as.integer(gsub(".*articles_(\\d{4})\\.parquet", "\\1", rf))
  outdir  <- file.path(cleaned_dir, paste0("year=", yr))
  outpath <- file.path(outdir, "part-0.parquet")
  if (file.exists(outpath)) return(paste0(yr, ": skip"))
  tryCatch({                                            # an OOM on one year must not abort the cluster
    dt <- as.data.table(arrow::read_parquet(rf))
    if (TEXT_COL %in% names(dt) && TEXT_COL != "article") setnames(dt, TEXT_COL, "article")
    if (!"article_id" %in% names(dt)) dt[, article_id := paste0(yr, "_", .I)]
    dt[, article := trimws(stringi::stri_replace_all_regex(article, "\\s+", " "))]
    dt[, word_count := stringi::stri_count_words(article)]
    dt[, ocr_nonalpha := { tot <- nchar(article)
                           fifelse(tot > 0, 1 - stringi::stri_count_regex(article, "[A-Za-z]") / tot, NA_real_) }]
    dt[, year := yr]
    before <- nrow(dt)
    dt <- dt[word_count >= MIN_WORDS & (is.na(ocr_nonalpha) | ocr_nonalpha <= MAX_NONALPHA)]
    dt <- unique(dt, by = "article")   # exact-duplicate text drop (syndicated boilerplate)
    dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
    tmp <- paste0(outpath, ".tmp"); arrow::write_parquet(dt, tmp); file.rename(tmp, outpath)
    sprintf("%d: %d -> %d kept (%.1f%%)", yr, before, nrow(dt), 100 * nrow(dt) / before)
  }, error = function(e) {
    tmpf <- paste0(outpath, ".tmp"); if (file.exists(tmpf)) unlink(tmpf)  # drop partial; year retries next run
    sprintf("%d: ERROR (%s)", yr, conditionMessage(e))
  })
}

# Resource-headroom policy: use <=80% of RAM/CPU, leave headroom for the OS and the parallel
# didgpu DiD work on this box. Never push for max speed -- a restart or RAM fight costs more than
# it saves. MEASURED (2026-05-26): a window year is ~3.8 GB raw and peaks ~25-35 GB in memory while
# cleaning (text normalization transiently doubles the article vector); 3 concurrent workers
# exhausted all 127 GB. So fix a SMALL, deterministic pool -- NO live WMI probe (Get-CimInstance
# hangs while WMI is busy and would stall the whole stage). 2 workers x ~30 GB ~= 60 GB (<50% RAM)
# leaves ample room to coexist with the DiD work. Override with CAPLAN_CLEAN_WORKERS if running solo.
NCLEAN_WORKERS <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("CAPLAN_CLEAN_WORKERS", "2"))
nworkers <- max(1L, min(NCLEAN_WORKERS, length(raw_files)))
message(sprintf("%d parallel clean workers (~30 GB/year peak; fixed for stability + coexistence)", nworkers))
cl <- parallel::makeCluster(nworkers)
parallel::clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(arrow); library(data.table); library(stringi)
  data.table::setDTthreads(2L)                          # avoid nworkers x 24-thread DT oversubscription
  arrow::set_cpu_count(2L); arrow::set_io_thread_count(2L)
})
parallel::clusterExport(cl, c("cleaned_dir", "TEXT_COL", "MIN_WORDS", "MAX_NONALPHA"), envir = environment())
print(unlist(parallel::parLapplyLB(cl, raw_files, clean_one)))   # LB: dispatch in order -> window-first
parallel::stopCluster(cl)
```

## Summary

```{r summary}
ds <- open_dataset(cleaned_dir)
library(dplyr)
ds |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(n = n(), med_words = median(word_count, na.rm = TRUE),
            med_nonalpha = median(ocr_nonalpha, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  collect() |> arrange(year) |> print(n = 100)
```
